data.title: Do Common Over-the-counter Antihistamine Medications Modify Thermoregulatory Responses During Passive Heat Stress?
data.brief_summary: Allergic rhinitis (AR) currently affects \~25% of Canadians, and due to factors of climate change, this number is expected to increase over the coming decade. AR symptoms can significantly impact individuals' quality of life by compromising sleep, productivity, and social interactions. To alleviate AR symptoms, North Americans tend to rely on H1 antihistamine medications available over-the-counter (OTC) at most pharmacies. * Able to provide informed consent
* Body-mass index under 30

Exclusion Criteria:

data.title: National Clinical Trial,Phase III, Multicenter, Randomized, Prospective, Double-blind, Parallel, Placebo-controlled, to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety and Superiority of Decongex Gripe in the Treatment of Symptoms Associated With Common Cold
data.brief_summary: National clinical trial, phase III, multicenter, randomized, prospective, double-blind, parallel, placebo-controlled, which one hundred and fifty (150) subjects of both sexes aged equal or more than 18 years will be randomly allocated to one the drug group or placebo group.
endpoints: Evaluation of the superiority of Desloratadine + Phenylephrine + Ibuprofen over placebo
